description_long: This is a heavily rusted, wedge-shaped hammer with a solid, tapered metal body. The handle is integrated into the same piece as the head, lacking any separate grip or ergonomic features, and appears cylindrical and slightly flared at the end. The head is broad and flat, gradually narrowing into the handle, with no visible claws or peen, suggesting it may be a primitive or specialized striking tool. The entire surface is covered in a dark brown, oxidized patina, indicating significant age and exposure to the elements. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements, and the object shows clear signs of corrosion and pitting. The hammer is compact and robust, likely intended for heavy-duty or demolition tasks in an urban or construction environment.
description: A compact, heavily rusted, wedge-shaped metal hammer with a flared handle and broad, flat head, showing significant corrosion and no visible markings.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the side, showing its wedge-shaped profile and the flared end of the handle.
description_view_1: The hammer is viewed from the top or bottom, highlighting the circular, flared end of the handle and the narrow width of the head.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the same wedge-shaped profile, with more visible rust and surface pitting.
description_view_3: The hammer is seen from an oblique angle, emphasizing the length of the handle and the tapering transition to the head.
attribute_handle_type: integrated iron handle, straight, cylindrical, flared at the end, rough and rusted surface, no grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: wedge-shaped iron head, broad and flat, no claw or peen, single striking face
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: extensive rust, pitting, and surface corrosion visible throughout
